title: 沉睡谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\140-沉睡谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Gwendolyn Reed makes a statement to Inspector Primer, claiming she saw a dead woman with golden hair and a purple face in a hillside villa hallway, identifying her as Helen. Gwendolyn suggests that someone framed her father for the murder of Lily Kimber, noting that Dr. Penrose and Dr. Kennedy believed her father was incapable of such an act. After Jules Reed is sent to the garden, Gwendolyn shares her speculations with the Inspector, including how Walter Fane and J.J. Avery received impersonated calls from Jules yesterday afternoon. Inspector Primer notes that Lily Kimber was murdered between 2:20 and 2:45 PM and emphasizes the need for verification rather than speculation.

In the garden, Inspector Primer meets Miss Marple, who is gardening. He acknowledges her reputation and her involvement in the case, specifically regarding the location of Mrs. Harriet's body. Miss Marple compares the deep roots of bindweed to the long-standing nature of the murder case. A policeman then announces that they have found a body.

As the discovery is made, Jules returns pale to inform Gwendolyn. Mrs. Coker comes into the garden and, upon seeing the remains, suffers a severe physical reaction. Gwendolyn provides her with brandy, but Mrs. Coker becomes unresponsive with terror and is eventually taken to the hospital. A forensic pathologist notes that Mrs. Coker might have died without medical intervention. Inspector Primer questions Gwendolyn about her recent consumption of alcohol, and she explains that she and Jules had been drinking whiskey instead of brandy. Following the police departure, Jervis eats lunch and joins the others, leaving Gwendolyn alone in the house.
